title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer has one blocking issue: the Helm `clusterrole.yaml` is missing RBAC rules for `policy/poddisruptionbudgets`, which will cause 403 errors at runtime. Two warnings suggest (1) backporting the improved reconcile pattern (owner refs, metadata merging, DeepEqual guard) from `EnsurePDB` to `EnsureDeployment`/`EnsureService` for consistency, and (2) explicitly setting `Replicas` in the PDB test so it doesn't silently depend on fixture defaults.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 65d3234 with Claude CLI

## BLOCKING (1)

### B-001: Missing RBAC ClusterRole rule for PodDisruptionBudgets
**File**: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: The kubebuilder RBAC marker was added to the controller for policy/poddisruptionbudgets, but the Helm ClusterRole template has no policy apiGroup rule. The operator will receive 403 Forbidden errors at runtime when attempting to create, get, or update PodDisruptionBudgets, silently breaking reconcileDeployment on every reconcile loop.

**Action Required**: Add the missing RBAC rule to clusterrole.yaml with apiGroups: [policy], resources: [poddisruptionbudgets], verbs: [get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete].

---

## CRITICAL (0)

No findings.

---

## WARNING (2)

### W-001: Inconsistent reconcile patterns across sibling Ensure functions
**File**: `internal/common/deployment/deployment.go`
**Actionability**: architectural
**Fix**: ASK

**Problem**: EnsurePDB introduces owner ref reconciliation, label/annotation merging on update, and DeepEqual guard before Update — behaviors that EnsureDeployment and EnsureService in the same file do not have. This behavioral divergence within the same package creates inconsistent expectations for developers.

**Action Required**: Consider backporting the improved pattern (owner ref reconciliation, metadata merging, DeepEqual guard) to EnsureDeployment and EnsureService in a follow-up PR.

### W-002: Test implicitly depends on fixture default replicas
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go:596`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: TestReconcileDeployment_PDBCreated uses deployTestKeystone() without setting Replicas explicitly. If the fixture default changes from Replicas: 3 to Replicas: 1, the test silently changes which PDB strategy path it exercises without failing.

**Action Required**: Explicitly set Replicas in the test fixture, consistent with the other PDB tests (TestReconcileDeployment_PDBMinAvailableForMultipleReplicas and TestReconcileDeployment_PDBMaxUnavailableForSingleReplica) which already do this.

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: No test for PDB creation failure propagation
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: There is no test verifying that when EnsurePDB returns an error, reconcileDeployment propagates it correctly. The existing test suite tests error propagation for EnsureDeployment and EnsureService failures but not for PDB.

**Action Required**: Add an analogous negative test for PDB error propagation to maintain coverage parity with EnsureDeployment and EnsureService failure tests.

### I-002: ClusterRole template out of sync with RBAC markers
**File**: `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/clusterrole.yaml`
**Actionability**: auto-fix
**Fix**: AUTO-FIX

**Problem**: The ClusterRole template serves as documentation of the operator's required permissions and is out of sync with the controller's kubebuilder RBAC markers, which now include policy/poddisruptionbudgets.

**Action Required**: Update the ClusterRole template to include the policy apiGroup rule, resolving this alongside the BLOCKING finding.

---

## Summary

| Category  | Count |
|-----------|-------|
| BLOCKING  | 1     |
| CRITICAL  | 0     |
| WARNING   | 2     |
| INFO      | 2     |

**Recommendation**: Do not merge until the BLOCKING RBAC ClusterRole issue is fixed. The missing policy apiGroup rule will cause runtime failures. The WARNING about implicit test fixture defaults should also be addressed before merge. The architectural inconsistency across Ensure functions can be addressed in a follow-up.
